framework.name: User Impersonation as Current Default Pattern
framework.description: OpenID confirms agents currently act indistinguishably from users rather than using delegated authority. This is the actual production pattern — not multi-hop delegation, but direct user impersonation. Explains why there are no delegation failures: nobody is delegating, they're impersonating.
framework.raw_quote: User impersonation by agents should be replaced by delegated authority. Currently, agents often act indistinguishably from users, creating accountability gaps and security risks.

source.title: [PDF] Identity Management for Agentic AI - OpenID
